automotive-ev-battery.title: Ultra-fast truck charging: the NEFTON Project boosts charging currents to 3,000A
automotive-ev-battery.summary: MAN and the Technical University of Munich have achieved a stable charging current of 3,000 amperes on test benches as part of the NEFTON project, representing significant progress toward megawatt-level charging infrastructure. This advancement is designed to enable charging power of up to 3MW, addressing a critical infrastructure gap for heavy-duty electric vehicles.

automotive-ev-battery.title: General Motors to prepare new EV platform
automotive-ev-battery.summary: General Motors is developing a new internal electric vehicle platform called 'BEV-N' to succeed its current BEV3 architecture, with first production vehicles expected by late 2028 or early 2029. This next-generation platform represents a significant strategic investment in GM's EV portfolio.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Digital battery passport test environment goes online
automotive-ev-battery.summary: A new test environment has launched to prepare companies for mandatory digital battery passports, which become required in February 2027 under EU regulations. Manufacturers, battery suppliers, and software companies can now test their data and applications for compliance and interoperability.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Nissan launches intelligent charging and V2G research project
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Nissan is participating in a research initiative exploring intelligent energy management and charging solutions for electric vehicles, including Vehicle-to-Grid technologies, solar integration, and AI-based energy systems. The project aims to advance the technical capabilities and grid integration potential of EVs.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Mercedes VLE first drive: Luxury electric travel with 800-volt technology and open windows
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Mercedes has introduced the VLE, an all-electric luxury people mover with seating for up to eight passengers, featuring 800-volt technology and rear-axle steering innovations. The vehicle represents Mercedes' strategic shift from its commercial van heritage toward premium electric passenger transport.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Orange EV reaches 2000 trucks milestone
automotive-ev-battery.summary: US electric truck manufacturer Orange EV has delivered its 2,000th battery-electric terminal truck to Coca-Cola Canada, expanding the beverage company's zero-emission fleet operations across British Columbia and Quebec. The milestone underscores growing commercial adoption of electric yard trucks in industrial logistics.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Rock Tech struggles with financing for lithium converter in Guben
automotive-ev-battery.summary: German-Canadian lithium processor Rock Tech Lithium has encountered financing difficulties for its planned converter facility in Guben, Brandenburg, according to statements made at a city council meeting. The project setback could impact European battery supply chain development plans.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Hyundai refines its virtual gearshift for electric vehicles
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Hyundai is developing an enhanced virtual gearshift system for its electric N performance models, incorporating simulated idle noises, vibrations, and exhaust sounds to replicate conventional driving experiences. The technology enhancement addresses driver engagement preferences in high-performance EVs.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Mazda starts exporting right-hand drive version of the 6e
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Mazda has commenced international shipments of the right-hand drive variant of its 6e electric vehicle, with initial market entry expected in Southeast Asia. The expansion demonstrates Mazda's growing commitment to the EV segment across diverse global markets.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Zoox Introduces Next-Generation Robotaxi
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Autonomous vehicle company Zoox has unveiled its next-generation robotaxi platform, marking a significant competitive development in the autonomous ride-hailing sector. The announcement positions Zoox alongside other major players like Waymo and Tesla in the emerging autonomous transportation market.

tech-ai.title: The White House is asking OpenAI to slow roll the release of its new model over safety concerns
tech-ai.summary: The Trump administration has requested that OpenAI delay the public release of its GPT-5.6 model, instead rolling it out to a limited group of enterprise partners due to security concerns. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman informed employees that the company will comply with the federal government's request during a preview period in which the administration will conduct its own security assessments.

tech-ai.title: Anthropic's Claude is winning over paid consumers, a market owned by ChatGPT
tech-ai.summary: Data shows that Anthropic's Claude is increasingly winning market share among paying AI consumers, despite OpenAI's ChatGPT maintaining a commanding overall market lead. The trend indicates a significant shift in consumer preference for paid AI services, with Claude gaining traction in a previously ChatGPT-dominated segment.

tech-ai.title: General Intuition's $2.3B bet that video games can train AI agents for the real world
tech-ai.summary: General Intuition has secured $320 million in funding to scale AI systems trained on millions of hours of video game footage, betting that action data from gaming can help AI develop intuitive decision-making capabilities closer to human reasoning. The company's approach represents a novel strategy for developing more generalizable AI agents.

tech-ai.title: Patronus AI lands $50M to build 'digital worlds' that stress-test AI agents
tech-ai.summary: Patronus AI, founded by former Meta AI researchers, has raised $50 million to develop synthetic testing environments that stress-test and validate AI agent performance. The funding reflects strong investor confidence in the startup's mission, as demand for reliable AI agent testing continues to grow rapidly.

tech-ai.title: Databricks' former AI chief thinks he can cut AI's power bill by 1,000x
tech-ai.summary: A former Databricks AI executive has developed Un-0, an image-generation system that demonstrates technology capable of replicating conventional AI systems while potentially reducing computational power consumption by up to 1,000 times. This breakthrough could significantly lower the operational costs associated with AI model deployment.

tech-ai.title: Notion Mail shuts down amid agent takeover
tech-ai.summary: Notion has discontinued its email inbox service as users increasingly adopt AI agents to manage their email communications. The shutdown reflects a broader trend of consumers delegating email management responsibilities to artificial intelligence systems.

tech-ai.title: RAMageddon just got extremely real
tech-ai.summary: Apple's widespread price increases across its product lines signal that the semiconductor supply crisis, particularly for RAM, has reached critical levels affecting the entire consumer tech industry. The company's historical ability to absorb cost fluctuations through volume purchasing suggests component shortages are severe enough that even Apple cannot weather them without raising prices.

tech-ai.title: Xbox follows Apple with price increases
tech-ai.summary: Microsoft has announced price increases across all Xbox console models, marking the second such increase in less than a year as memory and storage component costs continue to surge beyond historical levels. The company cited rising semiconductor expenses as the primary driver of the pricing adjustments.

tech-ai.title: Android 17's new foldable gaming mode could make flippy phones more fun
tech-ai.summary: Google is introducing a dedicated gaming mode for Android 17 foldable devices that displays a virtual gamepad with touch controls on half the screen to enhance gaming experiences. The feature, designed to work with any game supporting physical controllers, is scheduled for launch in the coming months.

tech-ai.title: a16z-backed Base Power is offering cheaper electricity to the power grid that needs it most
tech-ai.summary: Base Power is deploying battery storage systems at residential locations to bypass congested interconnection queues while providing backup power services to the grid. The approach offers a novel solution to infrastructure bottlenecks while potentially delivering cost savings to the electrical grid.

world-news.title: Rescuers search rubble for survivors as Venezuela earthquakes kill at least 235
world-news.summary: Two consecutive earthquakes measuring 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude struck Venezuela, killing at least 235 people and injuring nearly 1,500. Rescue operations are ongoing as survivors remain trapped beneath collapsed buildings across the country.

world-news.title: UN: Venezuela earthquakes will deepen already severe humanitarian crisis
world-news.summary: The United Nations announced it is scaling up humanitarian response efforts following the devastating twin earthquakes in Venezuela, warning that the disaster will exacerbate an already critical humanitarian situation in the country.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Israel attacks Lebanon as Netanyahu says troops to stay
world-news.summary: Israeli forces launched attacks on Lebanon as Prime Minister Netanyahu declared that military operations will continue in occupied southern Lebanese territories indefinitely, escalating regional tensions amid broader Middle Eastern conflict.

world-news.title: Trump asks Congress for billions for Iran war, after tension with Republicans
world-news.summary: President Trump requested substantial congressional funding for military operations against Iran, but the budget proposal faces significant opposition from members of his own party. The request highlights divisions within Republican leadership over foreign military spending.

world-news.title: France seizes fifth Russian 'shadow fleet' tanker linked to Ukraine war
world-news.summary: France has seized its fifth Russian shadow fleet tanker as part of enforcement efforts to cut off funding mechanisms for the Ukraine conflict. The continued seizures demonstrate escalating Western action against Russia's circumvention of sanctions.

world-news.title: Supreme Court allows Trump to end protected status for Haitian and Syrian immigrants
world-news.summary: The U.S. Supreme Court upheld a Trump administration decision to terminate protected status for Haitian and Syrian immigrants, potentially opening the way for deportations of hundreds of thousands of immigrants with long-term U.S. residency. The ruling represents a significant shift in U.S. immigration policy.

world-news.title: UN pauses Strait of Hormuz evacuation plan after cargo ship attacked
world-news.summary: A cargo vessel was struck by an unknown projectile near Oman in the strategically critical Strait of Hormuz, prompting the UN to temporarily suspend its evacuation operations. No casualties were reported in the incident, but the attack underscores security threats in one of the world's most important shipping corridors.

world-news.title: Paris restricts alcohol consumption and sales as Europe's heatwave shifts east
world-news.summary: French authorities implemented restrictions on alcohol sales and consumption in Paris as a heat emergency response, while temperatures in Germany are projected to reach 40 degrees Celsius. The extreme weather poses severe public health risks across continental Europe.

world-news.title: Who will control Africa's AI infrastructure, and at what cost?
world-news.summary: Rapid expansion of artificial intelligence data centers and systems across Africa is creating significant pressure on regional energy resources and raising geopolitical questions about infrastructure control and development priorities. The growth presents both economic opportunities and potential exploitation risks for the continent.

world-news.title: Families lay flowers on barbed wire barricade on anniversary of deadly Kenya protests
world-news.summary: Families of victims gathered to commemorate the deadly 2024 Kenya protests, demanding accountability from authorities for the deaths that occurred during demonstrations. The ongoing memory of the violence reflects broader questions about governance and security force conduct in East Africa.

science-space.title: James Webb Space Telescope catches 6 galaxies merging into one of the largest galaxies in the universe
science-space.summary: The James Webb Space Telescope has observed six galaxies in the process of merging into one of the universe's largest galaxies, with a supermassive black hole forming at its center. This discovery provides unprecedented insights into galaxy formation and the role of supermassive black holes in cosmic evolution.

science-space.title: Two 'super-puff' cotton candy exoplanets are the lightest gas giants ever discovered
science-space.summary: Astronomers have discovered two exceptionally low-density exoplanets orbiting the same star with densities comparable to cotton candy, establishing a new record as the lightest gas giants ever observed. These unusual 'super-puff' worlds challenge existing models of planetary formation and composition.

science-space.title: Millions of Stars in Cigar Galaxy
science-space.summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope has captured new infrared observations of the edge-on starburst galaxy Messier 82, revealing millions of stars while penetrating dust clouds invisible to previous telescopes. Combined with archival Hubble data, this comprehensive view provides unprecedented detail of this active galactic system.

science-space.title: Base editing reveals an essential role for NANOG in human embryogenesis
science-space.summary: Researchers using base editing technology have identified NANOG as an essential factor in human embryonic development, advancing understanding of the genetic mechanisms governing early human development. This finding has significant implications for developmental biology and potential therapeutic applications.

science-space.title: NASA Selects Rocket Lab to Launch Sun, Earth Science Missions
science-space.summary: NASA has selected Rocket Lab to provide launch services for the PolSIR and TSIS-2 science missions under the agency's VADR contract framework. These selections represent NASA's continued investment in commercial launch providers for critical Earth and solar observation missions.

science-space.title: NASA Welcomes Botswana as 68th Artemis Accords Signatory
science-space.summary: Botswana has become the 68th nation and the sixth African country to sign the Artemis Accords, joining a growing international community committed to peaceful and responsible space exploration. The accord represents expanding global cooperation in establishing norms for lunar exploration and space activities.

science-space.title: Expedition 73 Crew Reflects on Science, Teamwork, and Life in Orbit
science-space.summary: Astronauts and cosmonauts from the International Space Station's Expedition 73 gathered at Space Center Houston to share experiences and insights from their missions in orbit. The welcome home ceremony recognized the contributions of space crews and the ground support teams essential to successful long-duration spaceflight.

science-space.title: La NASA compartirá los últimos avances del programa Base Lunar
science-space.summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman will deliver a virtual presentation on June 30 to discuss the latest developments in the agency's plans to establish a lunar surface base. The briefing will provide updates on NASA's strategy for sustained human presence and operations on the Moon.

science-space.title: NASA, US Small Business Administration to Announce Partnership
science-space.summary: NASA and the U.S. Small Business Administration will sign a memorandum of agreement to establish a new interagency initiative supporting the growth of the American space industry. The partnership directly aligns with national space policy objectives and aims to expand opportunities for small businesses in space commerce.

science-space.title: A SpaceX rocket will slam into the moon this August. Will we be able to see it?
science-space.summary: A spent SpaceX Falcon 9 upper stage is scheduled to impact the lunar surface in August, providing an opportunity to study rocket impacts on the Moon. Scientists are uncertain whether the collision will produce visible effects observable from Earth, warranting close observation by ground and orbital instruments.

finance-markets.title: Apple increases MacBook and iPad prices by 20%
finance-markets.summary: Apple has raised MacBook and iPad prices by 20 percent, citing elevated costs driven by an artificial intelligence-related memory chip shortage. The price increase announcement triggered a significant market reaction, with Apple losing $263 billion in market capitalization.

finance-markets.title: Micron shares jump as blockbuster results spark Wall Street tech shake-up
finance-markets.summary: Micron Technologies delivered blockbuster financial results that sparked a broader technology sector shift, with the company's shares surging while Apple's stock declined amid memory chip cost pressures. The results underscore the heightened volatility and shifting dynamics within the semiconductor and tech hardware markets.

finance-markets.title: Dimon upends succession race at JPMorgan again
finance-markets.summary: JPMorgan Chase Chief Executive Jamie Dimon has reshuffled the bank's succession plan, informing longtime executive Marianne Lake that she is no longer being considered for the top role as new deputies are elevated. The move represents another significant shift in the closely-watched leadership transition at the nation's largest bank.

finance-markets.title: Attack in Strait of Hormuz halts evacuation plan for stranded ships
finance-markets.summary: An International Maritime Organization evacuation effort for stranded vessels has been suspended following an attack on a container ship by an unknown projectile in the Strait of Hormuz. The incident raises renewed concerns about maritime security and safe passage through the strategically critical waterway.

finance-markets.title: Oil Holds Gain as Traders Weigh Hormuz Flows After Ship Attack
finance-markets.summary: Oil prices maintained gains following the attack on a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz, as traders assess potential disruptions to the vital shipping lane. The incident has sustained concerns about energy supply security and prompted reassessment of transit risks through the region.

finance-markets.title: Gold Steadies Near $4,000 as Inflation Data Eases Rate-Hike Bets
finance-markets.summary: Gold prices stabilized near the $4,000 per ounce level as softer-than-expected inflation data reduced expectations for near-term interest rate increases. The moderation in bullion volatility marked a stabilization following a turbulent week that saw prices reach their lowest level since November.

finance-markets.title: Competition intensifies for Anthropic and OpenAI ahead of IPOs
finance-markets.summary: Anthropic and OpenAI face intensified competitive pressure from open-source artificial intelligence models as both companies prepare for potential initial public offerings. The heightened competition raises critical questions about the valuation and market positioning of the leading AI laboratories.

finance-markets.title: Bayer wins crucial US Supreme Court ruling over Roundup weedkiller
finance-markets.summary: Bayer secured a significant Supreme Court victory after the justices overturned a verdict that had formed the basis for billions of dollars in claims alleging that Roundup weedkiller caused cancer. The ruling substantially reduces litigation risk and potential financial exposure for the chemicals company.

finance-markets.title: Trump Touts $11.1 Billion Aid Request to Woo Struggling Farmers
finance-markets.summary: President Trump announced a request to Congress for $11.1 billion in agricultural assistance at a White House dinner with farmers, representing the second major bailout of the sector this year. The aid package reflects continued challenges facing the agriculture industry and government fiscal priorities.

finance-markets.title: Cathay Bank Chairman Quits After Governance Dispute, Family Feud
finance-markets.summary: Cathay United Bank Chairman Kuo Ming-jian resigned following a regulatory breach at the lender's asset management affiliate and an incident involving a physical altercation with a member of Taiwan's billionaire Tsai family. The departure highlights governance challenges and family tensions at the Taiwan-based financial institution.

crypto-web3.title: Asset management giant Invesco files for tokenized fund targeting stablecoin reserve market
crypto-web3.summary: Invesco, a $2.5 trillion asset manager, has filed to launch a tokenized money market fund investing in U.S. Treasuries, repo agreements, and cash equivalents. This move represents a significant institutional commitment to blockchain-based asset management and follows Invesco's earlier acquisition of Superstate's tokenized fund operations.

crypto-web3.title: Senate races to advance crypto legislation in July as housing bill turmoil threatens timeline
crypto-web3.summary: U.S. Senate faces mounting pressure to advance comprehensive cryptocurrency legislation in July as procedural complications with housing bills create scheduling challenges. The sweeping crypto bill's passage is increasingly uncertain amid a narrowing legislative window.

crypto-web3.title: Kraken in talks to buy 15% stake in DeFi lender Aave at $385 million valuation
crypto-web3.summary: Cryptocurrency exchange Kraken is negotiating to acquire a 15% stake in Aave, the prominent DeFi lending protocol, valuing the firm at $385 million. Aave is rebuilding its user base following an April exploit linked to KelpDAO that triggered a multi-billion-dollar deposit exodus.

crypto-web3.title: Aave founder Stani Kulechov says AAVE isn't for sale 'at a 70% discount' following report of Payward bid
crypto-web3.summary: Aave founder Stani Kulechov publicly dismissed acquisition overtures, stating the protocol is not for sale at a significant discount following news of external bidding interest. The statement comes after the protocol redirected all revenue to token holders through the Aave Will Win proposal.

crypto-web3.title: Coinbase's Base blockchain resumes after two-hour outage disrupted network
crypto-web3.summary: Coinbase's Base layer-2 network experienced a significant two-hour outage that halted transaction processing and affected deposits and withdrawals. The incident highlighted infrastructure vulnerabilities in one of Ethereum's largest scaling solutions.

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin tumbles to new multi-year low of $58,000, but a short-squeeze setup emerges
crypto-web3.summary: Bitcoin declined to a multi-year low of $58,000 as market pessimism deepened, yet derivatives data suggests an overcrowded bearish positioning that could trigger a sharp short-squeeze rally. The technical setup indicates potential for significant price recovery despite bearish market sentiment.

crypto-web3.title: Quant fund says bitcoin is near a major inflection point as rare onchain signals align
crypto-web3.summary: Quant fund manager Hyperion Decimus reports that four historically reliable on-chain indicators have simultaneously aligned, suggesting bitcoin stands at a critical technical juncture. According to analyst Chris Sullivan, the convergence of these signals positions bitcoin for a major directional move.

crypto-web3.title: a16z-backed crypto firm rebrands, shifts focus to solving AI's global copyright headache
crypto-web3.summary: Story Protocol, a Sequoia and a16z-backed startup, has rebranded and raised $140 million to build an audit layer for data consent, licensing, and provenance in AI and technology. The firm is positioning itself as a critical infrastructure provider for managing intellectual property rights in the AI era.

crypto-web3.title: Tether-pegged USDT0 stablecoin crosses $100 billion transaction volume milestone
crypto-web3.summary: USDT0, a Tether-pegged stablecoin backed 1:1 by USDT, has surpassed $100 billion in cumulative transaction volume, becoming the third-largest holder of Tether. The milestone reflects growing adoption of wrapped stablecoin solutions.

crypto-web3.title: Multicoin predicts HYPE hits $319 by 2028 as Hyperliquid turns into an 'everything exchange'
crypto-web3.summary: Investment firm Multicoin Capital projects Hyperliquid's HYPE token will reach $319 by 2028 as the platform evolves into a comprehensive exchange infrastructure. Hyperliquid's user base has grown substantially from 300,000 to 923,000 users during 2025.

health-biotech.title: Merck KGaA makes largest deal in a decade in $11.3B buy for Bio-Techne
health-biotech.summary: Merck KGaA announced its largest acquisition in a decade, acquiring Bio-Techne for $11.3 billion, signaling significant consolidation activity in the biotech sector. The deal reflects ongoing industry consolidation and strategic positioning in life sciences research tools.

health-biotech.title: After buying Apellis for $5.6B, Biogen is cutting most of its pipeline work
health-biotech.summary: Biogen has paused or canceled the majority of research programs acquired through its $5.6 billion purchase of Apellis Pharmaceuticals and conducted workforce reductions. The move highlights challenges in biotech acquisition integration and strategic portfolio management.

health-biotech.title: Next-gen CRISPR tools improve editing accuracy in embryos, but also stoke ethical concerns
health-biotech.summary: Advanced CRISPR base-editing tools have demonstrated improved accuracy in human embryo editing while illuminating the role of key genes in early human development. The breakthrough raises significant ethical and regulatory questions about the use of genome editing in embryos.

health-biotech.title: Democrats press White House on who got special access to Eli Lilly's new obesity drug
health-biotech.summary: Congressional Democrats are investigating the unusual access granted to select individuals for Eli Lilly's experimental obesity drug, raising questions about fairness and transparency in drug development. The controversy has garnered significant public attention and regulatory scrutiny.

health-biotech.title: New ACIP charter broadens criteria for members, calls for review of alternatives to vaccines
health-biotech.summary: The Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices has adopted a new charter that substantially refocuses its responsibilities and downplays its role in recommending new vaccines while broadening membership criteria. The changes signal a shift in how vaccine recommendations will be evaluated and approved.

health-biotech.title: FDA's breakthrough pipeline fills up with generative AI devices
health-biotech.summary: The FDA has accelerated breakthrough designations for generative AI-based medical devices, while also dropping enforcement action against the biometric wearable company Whoop. The trend reflects growing regulatory support for AI-driven healthcare innovation.

health-biotech.title: Proposed CDC science office could tighten political control at agency
health-biotech.summary: The Trump administration is proposing a new CDC science office that would increase political oversight of the agency's research and recommendations. The initiative raises concerns about the independence and scientific integrity of federal health agency decision-making.

health-biotech.title: Autoimmune disease company Oblenio Bio clinches $62M in Series B
health-biotech.summary: Oblenio Bio secured $62 million in oversubscribed Series B funding led by Pfizer Ventures to advance a trispecific T-cell engager for autoimmune diseases into human trials. The raise reflects investor confidence in novel immunotherapy approaches for autoimmune conditions.

health-biotech.title: Antares gets $105M from Novartis for new cancer drugs
health-biotech.summary: Novartis invested $105 million in Scorpion Therapeutics spinout Antares to accelerate development of novel cancer therapeutics through a collaborative partnership. The deal demonstrates Big Pharma's continued reliance on biotech partnerships to advance oncology pipelines.

health-biotech.title: Cassidy proposes bill to rein in 340B drug discount program
health-biotech.summary: Senator Cassidy has introduced legislation to impose new restrictions on the 340B drug discount program, a proposal that would create additional financial pressures for hospitals already facing significant funding constraints. The bill aims to address concerns about the program's expansion and utilization.

startups-vc.title: General Intuition's $2.3B bet that video games can train AI agents for the real world
startups-vc.summary: General Intuition has raised $320 million in funding to scale AI trained on millions of hours of video gameplay, positioning action data as a pathway to develop AI with human-like intuition. The company's approach represents a significant bet on using gaming simulations to create more capable real-world AI agents.

startups-vc.title: AppsFlyer Reportedly Lands $1B At $2.7B Valuation To Help Companies Track Digital Ads
startups-vc.summary: Digital analytics company AppsFlyer has secured over $1 billion in Series E funding at a $2.7 billion valuation as it eyes a public market exit. The funding round underscores strong investor appetite for data analytics platforms serving the digital advertising sector.

startups-vc.title: Cursor Deal Puts US On Track For Record Startup M&A Year
startups-vc.summary: The U.S. startup ecosystem is on pace for record M&A activity in 2026, highlighted by SpaceX's $60 billion acquisition of Anysphere and multiple other multi-billion-dollar transactions. The article examines the ten largest M&A deals completed year-to-date.

startups-vc.title: SaaS Isn't Coming Back. Something Much Bigger Is Replacing It
startups-vc.summary: The next generation of software will be driven by AI-native, industry-specific platforms rather than horizontal SaaS products, according to industry analysis. Vertical AI companies with deep domain expertise and proprietary data are positioned to capture the largest market opportunities due to durable competitive advantages.

startups-vc.title: European Investor Seedcamp Closes On $320M Across Two Funds To Back Seed Startups And Reaches $1B AUM
startups-vc.summary: Seedcamp, a leading European seed-stage investor, has closed its seventh fund with $220 million plus an additional select fund of $100 million, bringing total assets under management to $1 billion. The fundraising demonstrates strong institutional backing for early-stage startup investing in Europe.

startups-vc.title: a16z-backed Base Power is offering cheaper electricity to the power grid that needs it most
startups-vc.summary: Base Power, backed by Andreessen Horowitz, is circumventing grid interconnection delays by deploying home-based batteries that provide backup services and cheaper electricity. The approach addresses infrastructure bottlenecks while creating a distributed energy solution.

startups-vc.title: Congratulations to EquipmentShare on Going Public
startups-vc.summary: EquipmentShare (YC W15), a construction equipment management platform, has completed its initial public offering after growing from Missouri to 373 locations nationwide. The company's IPO represents a significant exit for a Y Combinator-backed startup focused on serving the construction industry.

startups-vc.title: PayPal Put Agentforce on 8,000 Leads a Month No Human Was Going to Call. Conversions Jumped 50%.
startups-vc.summary: PayPal demonstrated significant results deploying Salesforce's Agentforce AI agent to manage 8,000 monthly leads, achieving a 50% conversion increase versus traditional sales processes. The case study exemplifies immediate practical applications of AI agents in enterprise sales operations.

startups-vc.title: What VCs Are Hunting Today: $100B in 10 Years
startups-vc.summary: Venture capital expectations have shifted dramatically, with investors now targeting $100 billion outcomes within a decade rather than the $1 billion exit valuations that once defined success for B2B software companies. The article reflects how market valuations and investor expectations have evolved over the past decade.

startups-vc.title: Announcing the YC AI Stack
startups-vc.summary: Y Combinator has partnered with nearly two dozen technology companies to provide students attending YC events with over $25,000 in complimentary credits for AI development tools. The initiative aims to lower barriers to entry for founders building AI-powered applications.

cybersecurity.title: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Zero-Day CVE-2026-20245 Exploited to Gain Root Access
cybersecurity.summary: A high-severity vulnerability in Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN was actively exploited by unknown threat actors as a zero-day for at least two months before public disclosure, allowing authenticated local attackers to execute arbitrary commands with elevated privileges. Google-owned Mandiant's findings underscore the significant risk window between active exploitation and vendor patch availability.

cybersecurity.title: Hackers Used Meta's AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
cybersecurity.summary: High-profile Instagram accounts including the Obama White House and U.S. Space Force Chief Master Sergeant were compromised after attackers circulated instructions on Telegram exploiting vulnerabilities in Meta's AI support assistant to reset account passwords. The incident demonstrates how AI-powered customer service systems can become attack vectors when improperly secured.

cybersecurity.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group 'The Gentlemen?'
cybersecurity.summary: The Gentlemen ransomware gang has rapidly ascended to become the second most active ransomware group by victim count, leveraging an aggressive recruitment strategy offering affiliates 90 percent of ransom proceeds. Krebs on Security investigates clues pointing to potential real-world identities of the group's administrator.

cybersecurity.title: New Gaslight macOS Malware Uses Prompt Injection to Disrupt AI-Assisted Analysis
cybersecurity.summary: A previously undocumented Rust-based macOS malware named Gaslight has been discovered incorporating prompt injection payloads designed to deceive AI-powered malware analysis tools into aborting investigations. This represents an emerging threat category where adversaries specifically target the AI systems that defenders rely upon for threat analysis.

cybersecurity.title: Apple's MacOS Gap Lets Users Disable Security Tools
cybersecurity.summary: A security vulnerability in macOS permits attackers to disable integrated security and browser tools without requiring administrator privileges or kernel exploits. This flaw significantly lowers the barrier for malicious actors to neutralize built-in protective mechanisms on Apple systems.

cybersecurity.title: Chrome Ad Blocker with 10M+ Installs Found with Dormant Script Injection Capability
cybersecurity.summary: A widely-distributed Chrome extension named Adblock for YouTube, featuring over 10 million installations and a Chrome Web Store featured badge, has been found to contain dormant capabilities for executing arbitrary JavaScript code. The discovery raises concerns about the security vetting process for popular browser extensions.

cybersecurity.title: New Mistic Backdoor Linked to KongTuke in ClickFix and ModeloRAT Campaigns
cybersecurity.summary: Symantec and Carbon Black researchers have identified a previously unknown stealthy backdoor named Mistic deployed in financially motivated attacks against organizations in insurance, education, IT, and professional services sectors since April 2026. The backdoor is linked to KongTuke, an initial access broker involved in ongoing ClickFix and ModeloRAT campaigns.

cybersecurity.title: Europe Evolves Into Ransomware's Favorite Region
cybersecurity.summary: Following a global lull in ransomware activity, cybercriminal groups have increasingly targeted European Union organizations and their supply chain partners, establishing the region as a preferred operational theater. The shift reflects both the geographic concentration of valuable targets and evolving threat actor tactics.

cybersecurity.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
cybersecurity.summary: Major software vendors including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla, and Oracle have released near-record volumes of security patches in May 2026, demonstrating that artificial intelligence tools are increasingly effective at identifying vulnerabilities in human-written code. The unprecedented patch cadence underscores the evolving vulnerability landscape and the importance of rapid patching cycles.

cybersecurity.title: More Malicious OpenClaw Skills Threaten AI Supply Chain
cybersecurity.summary: OpenClaw removed five malicious packages from its ClawHub skills marketplace that successfully bypassed security checks while containing infostealers and other threats, highlighting vulnerabilities in AI supply chain security controls. The incident demonstrates the emerging risk of compromised AI skills and extensions as attack vectors.

energy-climate.title: China Briefing 25 June 2026: Five-year plans passed | Critical-mineral tensions | Industrial decarbonisation plan
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief's analysis examines China's latest five-year planning cycle, highlighting critical developments in mineral supply chains and the country's industrial decarbonization strategy. These policy decisions are significant given China's central role in global renewable energy supply chains and climate commitments.

energy-climate.title: Analysis: UK sales of electric vehicles just overtook petrol cars for the first time
energy-climate.summary: New electric vehicle sales in the United Kingdom have surpassed petrol vehicle sales for the first time, marking a historic milestone in the country's automotive transition. This crossover point demonstrates accelerating consumer adoption of battery electric vehicles and the maturation of the EV market.

energy-climate.title: Guest post: How US renewable-energy growth persists despite federal policy uncertainty
energy-climate.summary: Analysis reveals that United States renewable energy deployment continues to expand robustly despite recent fluctuations in federal energy policy, suggesting underlying market momentum and state-level support sustain sector growth. The findings demonstrate renewable energy's economic resilience independent of federal policy direction.

energy-climate.title: Australia selects 15 BESS projects totalling 4.2GW
energy-climate.summary: The Australian government has selected 15 battery energy storage system projects with combined capacity of 4.2GW and 16.1GWh through the Critical Infrastructure Stimulus Tender 8. This substantial investment supports grid stability and renewable energy integration across the region.

energy-climate.title: Impacts of the EU inverter ban
energy-climate.summary: The European Commission is restricting EU funding for solar projects using Chinese-made inverters due to cybersecurity concerns, prompting questions about European manufacturers' capacity to scale production and compete with China's market dominance. This policy shift has significant implications for European solar supply chains and competitive dynamics.

energy-climate.title: Latin America's clean power success creates a harder problem
energy-climate.summary: Grid constraints, energy storage capacity, and power offtake agreements have emerged as the critical limiting factors for the next phase of renewable energy investment in Latin America. The region's renewable deployment success has revealed infrastructure bottlenecks that must be addressed to sustain growth.

energy-climate.title: North Macedonia prepares to integrate gigawatt-sized project pipeline
energy-climate.summary: North Macedonia has developed a multi-gigawatt pipeline of solar and storage projects that is poised for accelerated deployment once regulatory permitting and grid integration challenges are resolved. The new permitting regime has temporarily slowed near-term development but positions the country for significant renewable capacity additions.

energy-climate.title: Romania on course for another record solar year
energy-climate.summary: Romania's solar and energy storage markets continue to expand at a rapid pace, supported by favorable market conditions and a supportive regulatory environment that is expected to deliver record installations. The country's sustained solar growth demonstrates the viability of the technology across diverse European markets.

energy-climate.title: Storage leading growth in Denmark's energy market
energy-climate.summary: Battery energy storage systems are emerging as the fastest-growing segment of Denmark's energy market, driven by electricity price volatility and favorable market signals across residential, commercial, and utility-scale segments. The trend reflects the critical role storage plays in enabling high renewable penetration.

energy-climate.title: Spain sets framework for new 1.2GW cogeneration tenders
energy-climate.summary: Spain's Council of Ministers has approved a Royal Decree to auction 1.2GW of cogeneration capacity, establishing the regulatory framework for new high-efficiency combined heat and power projects. This initiative supports Spain's broader energy transition goals through more efficient thermal generation.

asia-pacific.title: Chinese man changes locks on vacant flat, sells it to unsuspecting couple for US$103,000
asia-pacific.summary:
A man in eastern China deceived long-time friends out of over 700,000 yuan (US$103,000) by changing the lock on a random vacant flat and posing as its owner to sell it to them.
Sun, a Shanghai resident who previously owned two shops that went out of business, had a history of serving time for fraud, according to the mainland media outlet Xinmin Evening News.
Following his release from prison in 2017, a married couple he had known for nearly three decades consistently provided him with meals,...

asia-pacific.title: China’s award-winning 6G tech turns every wall and pipe into a sensor
asia-pacific.summary:
China’s 6G engineers have built a special metasurface system that can bounce wireless signals around like a precision mirror to fill in dead spots, while still sensing people moving about – just like a radar.
The innovation earned a gold award at the International Exhibition of Inventions in Geneva in March, the online results of which were announced on June 14.
The innovation is named Distributed Integrated Sensing and Communication Metasurface (DISACM). It uses intelligent reconfigurable...

asia-pacific.title: ‘Do or die’ for small carmakers: China market braces for 156 new models as price war looms
asia-pacific.summary:
A record 156 new models are expected to hit the Chinese automotive market in the second half of 2026, intensifying concerns about small players’ profitability as a new price war looms.
The mass-market segment would see an influx of intelligent electric vehicles (EVs) priced at around 100,000 yuan (US$14,740), but only leading carmakers were expected to secure large orders thanks to their brand awareness and production advantages, analysts said.
“Demand for new cars has rebounded but it is not...

asia-pacific.title: How Chinese philosophy influenced US founding fathers
asia-pacific.summary:
“Founding Father, Benjamin Franklin, published the sayings of Confucius in his colonial newspaper and today’s sculpture recognising that ancient Chinese age is carved into the face of the United States Supreme Court very proudly,” said US President Donald Trump in Beijing last month.
It took two-and-a-half centuries for an American president to explicitly acknowledge the profound Chinese impact on the US founding fathers. Trump’s recent declaration could be a historical first. Unless archival...

asia-pacific.title: Hong Kong’s own drug regulator pushes ahead with hiring, seeks WHO recognition
asia-pacific.summary:
As Hong Kong marks the 29th anniversary of its return to Chinese rule on July 1, the South China Morning Post talks to the city’s senior officials about the administration’s achievements so far and what may lie ahead.
Hong Kong is on a global talent hunt for its own drug approval body which is expected to be launched by the fourth quarter of this year as the city bids to achieve its international biomedical hub ambitions, the city’s health minister has revealed.
In an exclusive interview with...

asia-pacific.title: North Korea’s border build-up opens a Seoul-UN Command rift
asia-pacific.summary:
A public row between Seoul and the US-led United Nations Command (UNC) has revealed a fresh fault line over how the two interpret North Korea’s recent border fortification activities.
South Korea views its arch-rival’s actions as a violation of the 1953 Korean war armistice agreement. The UNC does not.
The dispute follows a separate disagreement over which authority should govern access to the demilitarised zone (DMZ), while analysts say differences also persist between Seoul and Washington over

asia-pacific.title: US Supreme Court paves way for Trump’s mass deportation of Haitians and Syrians
asia-pacific.summary:
The US Supreme Court on Thursday backed a Trump administration move to strip deportation protections from some 350,000 Haitians and 6,000 Syrians living in the United States.
The conservative-dominated court, in a 6-3 ruling, said the Department of Homeland Security’s decision to end Temporary Protected Status (TPS) for Haitian and Syrian immigrants was not subject to judicial review.
TPS protects its holders from deportation and is granted to people deemed to be in danger if they return home...

asia-pacific.title: IPO Connect pitched as Hong Kong’s next move to boost finance hub role. But hurdles loom
asia-pacific.summary:
Hong Kong is seizing every opportunity to consolidate its role as a global financial centre, leveraging unique advantages and national strategies. This piece, the second in a miniseries focusing on the city’s financial industry ahead of the handover anniversary, explores the likelihood of an “IPO Connect” that could inject much-needed liquidity into the market and deepen international investors’ access to top-tier China assets.
At every summer’s Lujiazui Forum, attended by China’s top financial.

asia-pacific.title: Venezuela earthquakes draw aid from governments that cut ties with Caracas
asia-pacific.summary:
Venezuela received offers of rescue teams and humanitarian aid from across the Americas on Thursday, including from right-wing governments that had broken off diplomatic relations with Caracas less than two years ago, after two earthquakes killed at least 188 people.
The quakes, of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 and 39 seconds apart, struck the north of the country on Wednesday evening and were the strongest to hit Venezuela in more than a century.
Interim President Delcy Rodriguez declared a nationwide.
